pi_items.source: Magento 2.4 official documentation confirms this is the correct configuration path for enabling Email to a Friend feature. Value '1' means enabled.

Magento 2.4 official documentation and source code (GitHub magento/magento2) confirm this is the correct configuration path. Value '0' means guests are not allowed to send emails (feature restricted to logged-in customers).
Magento 2.4 source code confirms this configuration path controls the maximum number of recipients per email. The value '3' is a reasonable restriction to prevent spam.
Magento 2.4 source code (config.xml) confirms this configuration path controls maximum products that can be shared in one hour. Value '5' matches Magento's own default value for this setting.
Magento 2.4 source code (app/code/Magento/Wishlist/etc/config.xml) confirms this configuration path. Default is '10', so '5' is a reasonable restriction.

Magento 2.4 documentation confirms email identities include: general (ident_general), sales (ident_sales), support (ident_support), custom1, custom2. The value 'support' refers to the Customer Support email identity (ident_support) as confirmed by GitHub issue #24419 and official Magento documentation.

privileged_info_summary: The task configures Magento 2 social sharing features via the core_config_data database table. All six configuration paths are verified as legitimate Magento 2.4 settings. The expected values are: Email to Friend enabled with guest access disabled and max 3 recipients/5 sends per hour; Wishlist sharing limited to 5 emails with Customer Support email identity as sender. These values represent reasonable security restrictions and anti-spam measures compared to Magento defaults.
